title: loxotrema pilosa
intro:
Loxotrema pilosa, commonly known as the hairy stinging tree, is a plant species known for its unique ability to cause intense pain and inflammation due to the presence of its needle-like hairs.

The health benefits of Loxotrema pilosa include its potential use in treating conditions such as arthritis, fibromyalgia, and other types of chronic pain, where the analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of the plant may be beneficial. 

The therapeutic actions of Loxotrema pilosa involve the release of histamine from mast cells, which can lead to increased blood flow and pain relief. The bioactive constituents of Loxotrema pilosa include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Loxotrema pilosa can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and dizziness due to its high toxicity levels. In severe cases, prolonged use or excessive consumption can lead to liver damage, kidney failure, and even respiratory issues. Some individuals may also experience allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and swelling, upon exposure to the plant's sap or oils. If ingested or applied topically without proper guidance, loxotrema pilosa can have severe and long-lasting consequences on one's health.
